berkshire_hathaway.non_us_notes: Berkshire's 13F covers nearly all public equity holdings. Non-US exposure is minimal in the equity portfolio — primarily through US-listed ADRs and some BYD shares held via a subsidiary. The vast majority of Berkshire's non-US value comes from wholly-owned operating subsidiaries (not public equities).

known_non_us_positions.company: Various Japanese trading companies (5 sogo shosha)

himalaya_capital.non_us_notes: Li Lu's Himalaya Capital has a massive position in BYD Company (China) acquired in 2002, now worth an estimated $10-15B+. He also holds Korean equities and other Asian positions. The 13F only captures a fraction of the total portfolio. Li Lu is known to be one of BYD's largest individual shareholders.

pabrai_funds.non_us_notes: Pabrai has largely shifted his portfolio to international markets. Last 13F was filed in 2011. He has invested heavily in Turkey (Atakey, Koc Holding, Turkiye Sise), India (Edelweiss Financial, Rain Industries), South Korea (Korea Zinc, Hyundai), and other emerging markets. His fund AUM is estimated around $1-2B.

baupost_group.non_us_notes: Baupost manages ~$25B total but much is in private investments, distressed debt, real estate, and non-public securities. The 13F public equity portfolio (~$5B) is primarily US-listed. Some non-US exposure through ADRs and international distressed situations but the public equity book is heavily US-focused. The large AUM gap is mostly private/illiquid assets, not non-US public equities.

tci_fund.non_us_notes: TCI Fund Management (Chris Hohn) is UK-based and runs a concentrated global portfolio. The 13F captures significant US holdings but TCI also holds major non-US positions. Historically held large positions in European infrastructure (Aena, London Stock Exchange Group) and other non-US names. Total AUM estimated at $60-70B+.

saber_capital.non_us_notes: Saber Capital is a US-based concentrated value fund. The 13F covers essentially 100% of the public equity portfolio. No known significant non-US positions.

akre_capital.non_us_notes: Akre Capital Management focuses almost exclusively on US-listed quality compounders. The 13F captures nearly the entire public equity portfolio. Minimal non-US exposure.

appaloosa_management.non_us_notes: Appaloosa Management (David Tepper) is primarily US-focused. The fund has ~$13B total AUM but includes credit/distressed positions not in the 13F. Some international exposure through US-listed ADRs (e.g., Alibaba historically). The non-equity portion is credit/distressed, not necessarily non-US public equities.

pershing_square.non_us_notes: Pershing Square (Bill Ackman) runs a concentrated portfolio primarily in US-listed large-cap stocks. The 13F covers most public holdings. Ackman has occasionally held non-US positions (e.g., CP Rail before merger). Pershing Square Tontine and Pershing Square Holdings (Amsterdam-listed) add some complexity but the core equity book is US-centric.

markel_gayner.non_us_notes: Markel Group (Tom Gayner) runs the equity portfolio for the Markel insurance company. Portfolio is almost entirely US-listed quality compounders. The 13F data in our system is stale (last from 2007 under old CIK). Markel's equity portfolio is disclosed in their 10-K and is ~$8B, primarily US stocks. Minimal non-US exposure.

cas_investment.non_us_notes: CAS Investment Partners (Cliff Sosin) runs an ultra-concentrated US portfolio. The 13F covers essentially 100% of public equity holdings. No known non-US positions.

oakcliff_capital.non_us_notes: Oakcliff Capital (Bryan Lawrence) is a concentrated US-focused quality value fund. The 13F captures essentially 100% of public equity holdings.

giverny_capital.non_us_notes: Giverny Capital (Francois Rochon) is a Canadian firm that files 13F for US holdings. The fund also holds Canadian-listed equities not captured by 13F. Rochon is known to hold some Canadian banks, TMX Group, and other Canadian names. Total AUM estimated at ~$4B CAD across multiple accounts.

fundsmith.non_us_notes: Fundsmith Equity Fund (Terry Smith) is a UK-based global fund with GBP 14.5B AUM (~$19B). The fund factsheet shows 68.1% US allocation and 31.9% non-US (France 12.1%, UK 7.5%, Denmark 4.7%, Spain 4.2%, Sweden 2.8%, Netherlands 0.6%). Known non-US holdings include L'Oreal, LVMH, Unilever, Novo Nordisk, and other European quality compounders. The 13F only captures US-listed positions.

known_non_us_positions.company: Other European holdings (Amadeus IT, Atlas Copco, etc.)

semper_augustus.non_us_notes: Semper Augustus (Christopher Bloomstran) is a US-focused deep value fund. The 13F captures essentially 100% of public equity holdings. Bloomstran's largest position is typically Berkshire Hathaway. No known significant non-US positions.

dorsey_asset.non_us_notes: Dorsey Asset Management (Pat Dorsey) focuses on US-listed wide-moat businesses. The 13F covers essentially 100% of public equity holdings. No known significant non-US positions.

gardner_russo.non_us_notes: Gardner Russo & Quinn (Thomas Russo) is one of the most internationally-oriented US-based value managers. Russo is famous for his 'global value' approach and 'capacity to reinvest' thesis. He holds major positions in European consumer companies not captured by 13F, including Nestle, Richemont (Cartier parent), Pernod Ricard, and other global brands. Total AUM estimated at ~$15B with roughly 35% in non-US names.

chou_associates.non_us_notes: Chou Associates (Francis Chou) is a Canadian deep value fund. The 13F captures US holdings but the fund also holds significant Canadian and international positions. Chou runs multiple funds (Chou Associates, Chou Bond, Chou Asia, Chou RRSP). The Canadian holdings and international positions are not in the 13F. Known for contrarian, distressed-style investing in Canadian and global markets.

harris_associates.non_us_notes: Harris Associates (Bill Nygren) manages the Oakmark Funds, including the Oakmark International Fund and Oakmark Global Fund. The 13F captures the US holdings but Harris also manages ~$15-20B in international equities through Oakmark International. The international fund holds European and Japanese stocks. Total firm AUM is ~$90B.

davis_advisors.non_us_notes: Davis Selected Advisers (Chris Davis) runs the Davis Funds including Davis International Fund. The 13F captures the bulk of US holdings. Davis has some international exposure through the Davis International Fund and global mandates, but the flagship Davis New York Venture Fund is primarily US-focused.

ruane_cunniff.non_us_notes: Ruane, Cunniff & Goldfarb (Sequoia Fund, David Poppe) is primarily US-focused. The 13F captures nearly all public equity holdings. Minimal international exposure, mostly through US-listed companies with global operations.

century_management.non_us_notes: Century Management / Van Den Berg Management (Arnold Van Den Berg) is a US-focused deep value firm. The 13F captures essentially 100% of public equity holdings.

horizon_kinetics.non_us_notes: Horizon Kinetics (Murray Stahl) is primarily US-focused but has some exposure to non-US royalty and real asset companies. Most holdings are US-listed. Minor non-US exposure through some international royalty companies and exchange operator stakes.

lone_pine.non_us_notes: Lone Pine Capital (Stephen Mandel) is a Tiger Cub hedge fund running long/short equity globally. The 13F captures the US long book but Lone Pine also holds non-US longs (especially in tech and consumer) and has a significant short book. Total AUM estimated at ~$20B. Known for holding positions in non-US tech/growth companies not captured by 13F.

fairfax_financial.non_us_notes: Fairfax Financial Holdings (Prem Watsa) is a Canadian insurance holding company with a global investment portfolio. The 13F only captures the US equity portion (~$2B), but Fairfax's total investment portfolio is ~$55B including bonds, equities, and subsidiaries globally. Major non-US holdings include Fairfax India Holdings (~$5B), significant Indian equity positions (ICICI Lombard, Quess Corp, CSB Bank, IIFL), Greek bank holdings (Eurobank), Canadian investments, and global insurance operations. The 13F massively understates Fairfax's total investment portfolio.

known_non_us_positions.company: Indian equities (ICICI Lombard, Quess, CSB Bank, IIFL)

atreides_management.non_us_notes: Atreides Management (Gavin Baker) is a US tech/growth focused fund. The 13F captures nearly all public equity holdings. Primarily invests in US-listed technology companies. Some exposure to non-US tech through US-listed ADRs but the core book is US-centric.

coatue_management.non_us_notes: Coatue Management (Philippe Laffont) is a Tiger Cub tech-focused hedge fund with ~$50B AUM across public and private investments. The 13F captures the US public equity book. Coatue also has private/venture investments and some non-US public equity positions, especially in Asian tech. However, the public equity book is predominantly US-listed.

punch_card.non_us_notes: Punch Card Management (Norbert Lou) runs an ultra-concentrated US-focused portfolio. The 13F covers essentially 100% of public equity holdings.

situational_awareness.non_us_notes: Situational Awareness LP (Leopold Aschenbrenner / Carl Shulman) is an AGI-thesis fund focused on AI infrastructure. Primarily invests in US-listed AI/data center/power companies. No 13F data available yet (new fund). The portfolio is expected to be predominantly US-listed given the focus on US AI infrastructure.

rv_capital.non_us_notes: RV Capital (Robert Vinall) is a Swiss-based concentrated quality compounder fund. Files 13F sporadically. Historically held a mix of US and European positions including Constellation Software (Canada), Amazon, and various European mid-caps. The fund is small (~$500M) and the majority of holdings are non-US listed.

lindsell_train.non_us_notes: Lindsell Train (Nick Train) is a UK-based fund manager with ~GBP 12B AUM. Ultra-low turnover, holds durable franchise businesses. Core holdings include London Stock Exchange Group, Unilever, Diageo, RELX, Mondelez, Nintendo, and other global consumer/financial brands. The majority of holdings are UK/European/Japanese listed. Files 13F but US positions are a minority.

arlington_value.non_us_notes: Arlington Value Capital (Allan Mecham) returned outside capital around 2020. No longer actively managing external money. Last 13F filed 2020-05-15. INACTIVE.
